figure.title: Isolation Mode Response
figure.purpose: Shows the system's reaction to a link failure by putting components into Isolation Mode.

technical_summary: Following a link down event, components on both sides of the failed link enter Isolation Mode.
The UPLI Originator and Completer in ACC A, and the corresponding UPLI Originator and Completer in the Switch, are highlighted with dashed orange boxes.

An annotation indicates: 'credited Write Response is issued from UALink TL to UADP Originator to put it into Isolation Mode.' Note: the block is labeled UPU Originator, suggesting UPU and UADP might be used interchangeably or represent related upper layers.

components.name: UPU Originator / UADP Originator
components.description: Upper layer component that receives a response to enter isolation mode.

critical_facts: In response to a link failure, the UALink TL issues a credited Write Response to the upper layer originator to place it into Isolation Mode.
potential_ambiguities: The diagram labels the block 'UPU Originator' while the text refers to 'UADP Originator'.
